channel.title: Sound on mlesniak.com
channel.description: Recent content in Sound on mlesniak.com

item.title: Go Guitar // Visualize Sound
item.description: After understanding how to record sound in Go I wanted to visualize the recorded data in realtime instead of having solely a static image. The graphics library SDL is available on all major platforms, well documented, and in particular has (at least) one go binding with good examples. The full code for this example can be found here. In this article I’ll focus on setting up and using SDL in Go and describe a pitfall I ran into.

item.title: Go Guitar // Record Sound
item.description: As part of my ongoing project to write a Go program to recognize different guitar chords and get a better understanding of sound processing, a first milestone has been reached: recording a raw sound sample in Go. In this article I’ll walk through the source code to record sounds using a wrapper for PortAudio and visualize the recorded sample in gnuplot. The whole source code for this blog post can be found in the corresponding GitHub repository.
